data.title: Institutional Outlook for the Fed's Interest Rate Decision: Rates Expected to Remain Unchanged
data.description: The following are institutional predictions for the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision, all of which believe the Fed will maintain the current rate: 1. Macquarie: Expects the Fed to keep rates unchanged; declining unemployment will guide the Fed to remain on hold. 2. Goldman Sachs: Expects the Fed to keep rates unchanged; the Fed will only make minor adjustments to its monetary policy stance. 3. Nomura: Expects the Fed to keep rates unchanged. Policy guidance will continue to indicate a higher threshold for future rate cuts. 4. Oxford Economics: Expects the Fed to keep rates unchanged. Economic growth will improve this year, and the Fed will maintain its current policy until June. 5. Citigroup: Expects the Fed to keep rates unchanged. The truly important signal will come from Powell's tone, especially the extent to which he opens the door to future rate cuts. 6. Barclays: Expects the Fed to keep rates unchanged; the combination of employment and inflation data does not support immediate further rate cuts, and the FOMC needs time to assess the impact of recent rate cuts. 7. Rabobank: Expects the Fed to keep rates unchanged. A majority of committee members believe that the three insurance rate cuts implemented at the end of last year were sufficient. 8. JPMorgan Chase: Expects the Fed to keep rates unchanged. The labor market has not deteriorated rapidly, and the recent stabilization of the unemployment rate should eventually lead the FOMC to reach a consensus to hold rates steady. 9. Morgan Stanley: Expects rates to remain unchanged. The statement is expected to revise its economic growth forecast from "moderate" to "solid" and remove the wording regarding "increased downside risks to employment." 10. KBC: Expects rates to remain unchanged. The downside risks to the labor market have eased, and real GDP growth is expected; these two factors together reduce the need for a rate cut at the January meeting. The Federal Reserve is scheduled to announce its interest rate decision at 3:00 AM on January 29th. (Jinshi)

data.title: The UK has banned Coinbase from publishing advertisements that downplay the risks of cryptocurrencies.
data.description: The UK Advertising Standards Authority (ASA) has banned a series of ads from Coinbase, arguing that they portrayed the cryptocurrency exchange as a solution to the cost of living while downplaying the risks of crypto investments. The ASA stated that the ads, including a satirical music video and three posters, were “irresponsible” and “downplayed the risks of cryptocurrencies.” They used humor to address serious financial problems and implied “change,” potentially misleading consumers into believing that complex, high-risk financial products were the simple or obvious solution. These ads had previously been rejected by Clearcast, the television advertising regulator, for similar reasons. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Thailand's Crackdown on Illegal Bitcoin Mining Operations
data.description: Thailand's special investigation force has uncovered a Bitcoin mining syndicate involving senior electricity officials accused of abusing their positions to conduct illegal mining operations. According to NS3.AI, thousands of mining rigs and substantial cash deposits were seized during raids conducted across multiple provinces as part of the ongoing 'Operation Copperhead'. This crackdown is part of Thailand's broader initiative to combat illicit cryptocurrency activities and aligns with similar global efforts to regulate unauthorized mining practices.

data.title: Japan's Financial Services Agency Seeks Public Input on Crypto Regulations
data.description:
Japan's Financial Services Agency has initiated a public consultation on draft implementation details concerning cryptocurrencies, electronic payment tools, and financial institutions. According to ChainCatcher, the draft outlines specific execution requirements following the 2025 revision of the Payment Services Act, including updates to official notices, administrative guidelines, and regulatory rules.

The draft covers various areas, such as the designation of new bonds as supporting assets, the regulatory framework for electronic payment tools and crypto-related intermediary services, and updated regulatory guidelines for financial institutions and their subsidiaries. The consultation will conclude on February 27, 2026, after which the regulations will take effect following the completion of necessary procedures, with results to be announced separately.

The Financial Services Agency is reportedly planning a comprehensive overhaul of the regulatory framework, aiming to introduce the country's first spot crypto ETFs by 2028. The roadmap includes reclassifying cryptocurrencies as 'specified assets' under the Investment Trust Law, reducing the crypto capital gains tax from a maximum of 55% to a flat 20%, and allowing time to strengthen custody and investor protection standards.

data.title: Japan's Financial Services Agency is soliciting public opinions on detailed regulations governing digital payments and cryptocurrencies.
data.description: Japan's financial regulator, the Financial Services Agency (FSA), has opened a public consultation on draft implementing rules concerning cryptocurrencies, electronic payment instruments, and financial institutions. The draft clarifies the specific implementation requirements following the revision of the Payment Services Act in 2025, including updated official announcements, administrative guidelines, and related regulatory rules. The draft covers several aspects, including the designation of bonds as underlying assets, a regulatory framework for electronic payment instruments and crypto-related intermediary services, and updated regulatory guidelines for financial institutions and their subsidiaries. The public consultation will continue until February 27, 2026. After the necessary procedures are completed, the relevant regulations will officially take effect, and the results will be announced separately. Furthermore, the FSA is planning to adjust the overall regulatory framework, aiming to launch Japan's first spot crypto ETFs in 2028. The roadmap also includes reclassifying cryptocurrencies as "specified assets" under the Investment Trust Act, pushing for a reduction in the crypto capital gains tax from a maximum of 55% to a uniform 20%, and reserving space for further strengthening custody and investor protection standards. (Cryptobriefing)

data.title: 2U2.ai builds an AI-driven meme infrastructure layer, reconstructing the meme culture production and dissemination chain.
data.description: 2U2.ai, a decentralized meme infrastructure incubated by DreamTech, a comprehensive creative production platform in Japan, has launched a decentralized meme production and distribution protocol, aiming to reconstruct the meme culture production and dissemination chain. Through AI-driven creation tools and native dissemination mechanisms, the 2U2.ai Meme Layer provides the community with scalable meme production capabilities, making memes not just content and culture, but also fundamental units driving consensus, attention, and value flow. In its initial launch phase, 2U2.ai has attracted over 100,000 users through a series of activities, and its product model has completed initial validation. Next, the project plans to launch an airdrop incentive mechanism and continuously iterate product features to further expand the application boundaries of memes in community expression, cultural narratives, and on-chain value synergy. The goal is to make memes more than just content; they will become a core infrastructure module of the on-chain ecosystem.

data.title: Ethereum Spot ETFs Experience Significant Net Outflows
data.description: Ethereum spot ETFs have seen a total net outflow of $63.53 million, according to ChainCatcher. Data from SoSoValue indicates that the Grayscale Ethereum Mini Trust ETF ETH recorded the highest single-day net inflow of $9.99 million yesterday, bringing its historical total net inflow to $1.653 billion. In contrast, the BlackRock ETF ETHA experienced the largest single-day net outflow of $58.97 million, with its historical total net inflow reaching $12.427 billion. As of the time of reporting, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs stands at $18.154 billion, with an ETF net asset ratio of 4.98% and a cumulative historical net inflow of $12.355 billion.

data.title: South Korea Proposes Licensing Framework for Crypto Exchanges
data.description: South Korea's Financial Services Commission (FSC) chairman, Lee Eok-won, has suggested transitioning from the current registration system for cryptocurrency exchanges to a formal licensing framework. According to NS3.AI, this proposal is part of the second phase of the Digital Asset Basic Act, which aims to enhance the regulatory framework and responsibilities of crypto trading platforms. The existing system mandates exchanges to renew their registrations every three years. However, the proposed licensing model seeks to establish a more robust legal foundation to increase market attractiveness.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $63.5334 million yesterday, with BlackRock's ETHA experiencing the largest net outflow at $58.9679 million.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $63.5334 million yesterday (January 27th, Eastern Time). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was the Grayscale Ethereum Mini Trust ETF (ETH), with a net inflow of $9.9868 million. ETH's historical total net inflow has reached $1.653 billion. The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was the BlackRock ETF (ETHA), with a net outflow of $58.9679 million. ETHA's historical total net inflow has reached $12.427 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs is $18.154 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Ethereum's total market capitalization) of 4.98%. The historical cumulative net inflow has reached $12.355 billion.

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $147 million yesterday, with BlackRock's IBIT experiencing the largest net outflow at $103 million.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $147 million yesterday (January 27th, Eastern Time). The BlackRock ETF (IBIT) experienced the largest single-day net outflow, at $103 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $62.816 billion. The Fidelity ETF (FBTC) followed with a net outflow of $44.5591 million, while its historical total net inflow is $11.415 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $114.989 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Bitcoin's total market capitalization) of 6.44%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $56.354 billion.

data.title: Vitalik Highlights Key Areas for AI and Crypto Integration
data.description: Vitalik, in an interview, discussed potential intersections between AI and cryptocurrency. According to Foresight News, he identified several promising areas for exploration. Firstly, AI's inability to open accounts with traditional banks makes cryptocurrency a viable option for AI agents needing funds to execute tasks. Secondly, AI can participate in prediction markets, offering more accurate information as traders. Lastly, blockchain technology can be used to verify whether content is created by humans or generated by AI.

data.title: Vitalik Buterin Critiques Short-Term Bets on Prediction Markets
data.description:
Ethereum founder Vitalik Buterin recently shared his views on prediction markets during an interview with ForsightNews. According to BlockBeats, Buterin criticized platforms like Polymarket for promoting short-term bets such as 'which team will win next week' or 'whether Bitcoin's price will rise or fall in an hour.' He argued that these short-term wagers lack significant social value in the long run.

Buterin emphasized that while prediction markets are theoretically successful as tools, there is a need for more meaningful applications. Reflecting on his own experience, he revealed that he used $440,000 as principal last year on Polymarket and earned $70,000. His strategy involved identifying markets that had entered a 'crazy mode' and betting against the occurrence of irrational events. This approach, he noted, often proved profitable when market sentiment became irrational.

data.title: Bitwise Takes Initial Steps Toward Uniswap-Linked ETF
data.description:
Bitwise has established a Delaware statutory trust as a preliminary move towards launching an ETF linked to Uniswap. According to NS3.AI, this action does not involve an immediate filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). The development comes after the SEC concluded its investigation into Uniswap Labs, indicating a shift in regulatory focus from enforcement to structural and liquidity aspects within the decentralized finance (DeFi) sector.

Industry experts highlight that custody and liquidity are crucial factors for the approval of such financial products. Uniswap’s UNI token continues to demonstrate strong trading volumes, although operational complexities remain a challenge. The evolving regulatory landscape suggests a growing interest in addressing these complexities to facilitate the integration of DeFi into traditional financial systems.

data.title: Vitalik Buterin Discusses Earnings and Prediction Market Challenges
data.description:
Vitalik Buterin revealed in an interview that he earned $70,000 last year on Polymarket, starting with a principal of $440,000. According to Foresight News, Buterin's strategy involved identifying markets that were in 'crazy mode' and betting that 'crazy things won't happen.' He focused on segments within Polymarket, including politics and technology.

Buterin also addressed potential oracle issues in prediction markets, suggesting two main solutions. The first is a centralized approach, which involves trusting a company, such as Bloomberg, to provide accurate information. The second is token voting, a decentralized method exemplified by UMA, although trust in UMA has recently declined. Buterin expressed hope for a better solution in the future.

data.title: Large Transfer of QNT Tokens Observed Between Anonymous Addresses
data.description: A significant transfer of QNT tokens has been recorded, involving two anonymous addresses. According to ChainCatcher, at 12:06, a total of 27,377.84 QNT tokens were moved from one anonymous address, beginning with 0x5560, to another, starting with 0xa0bA. The transaction highlights ongoing activity within the cryptocurrency market, emphasizing the movement of substantial amounts of digital assets between unidentified parties.

data.title: A whale spent $3 million to buy 90,700 HYPE tokens.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a DeBank whale named "Einstein" (0xc7B...C2Ff) withdrew 3 million USDC from Lighter and deposited them into Hyperliquid, then bought 90,780 HYPE at a price of $33.05.
